storyboard.content: I've been having tingling and numbness in my hands, especially in the mornings. It feels like pins and needles but it doesn't go away quickly.
I also get the same tingling in my feet, especially in the mornings.
My fingers feel clumsy and I've been dropping things more often than usual — like I just can't grip properly. It's gotten noticeably harder to do things that require fine motor skills.
This has been gradually getting worse over the past 4 months. It didn't come on suddenly — it just slowly crept up on me.
It's affecting things I do every day, like typing and buttoning my clothes. I can still do most tasks but I have to work harder at them and it slows me down.
The tingling is different from when your foot normally falls asleep — it doesn't go away quickly. It lingers, especially in the mornings and after I've been sitting for a while.
The symptoms seem to get worse when I've been sitting still for a long period of time. Moving around doesn't seem to make it much better.
I'm a 47-year-old woman. I don't have any diagnosed medical conditions that I know of, and I'm not on any regular medications.
